metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the preamble injection strategy covers `plan-review-verifier` at its actual runtime call site (the workflow gate step), not only via `_resolve_system`, since passes.py:218-221 confirms the verifier no longer goes through `_resolve_system` at runtime.
findings.finding: PASS_VERIFIER (`plan-review-verifier`) is explicitly NOT routed through `_resolve_system` at runtime. The comment at passes.py:218-221 states the bespoke pass2_verify call 'was retired in epic solid-timer-unison'; the verifier now runs via the workflow gate's prompt step, bypassing `_resolve_system`. Injecting the shared preamble into `_resolve_system` will therefore NOT reach the verifier prompt, yet AC1 asserts 'every plan_review reviewer resolves with the preamble prepended' and the proof test is named `resolve_system_preamble`.
findings.suggested_fix: Identify where the workflow gate resolves the verifier prompt (likely via `prompts.resolve_prompt` or a RunnerAgentStep config) and apply the preamble injection there too, or unify all passes back through `_resolve_system` for the preamble step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the G-12 + FP-3(c) preamble reaches `plan-review-verifier` and `plan-review-verifier-agentic`, which are served via workflow gate `prompt:` steps (plan-review.yaml:144,234) that bypass `_resolve_system`, and update AC1's proof to cover the workflow-step resolution path.
findings.finding: The plan injects the shared preamble (G-12 + FP-3(c)) via _resolve_system, but the two verifier prompts — plan-review-verifier and plan-review-verifier-agentic — are NOT routed through _resolve_system at runtime. They are served as workflow gate `prompt:` steps (plan-review.yaml:144 and :234), bypassing the _resolve_system hook entirely. AC1's pytest assertion (`resolve_system_preamble`) will pass for the five prompts that use _resolve_system but will NOT exercise the verifier path, leaving the passes that are most exposed to plan-content injection (Pass-2 receives the full plan text) without the G-12 anti-injection directive.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) prepend the preamble text directly into plan_review_verifier.md and plan_review_verifier_agentic.md as part of this story's sweep, and add a deterministic test that reads those files and asserts the preamble strings are present; or (b) extend the plan to acknowledge this path is out of scope with a documented rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC1's proof to also assert that plan_review_F1.md no longer contains the FP-3(c) forward-looking rule after the hoist (e.g., a grep/scripted check analogous to the bare-DO-NOT count in AC2), so the migration verifies both removal and replacement.
findings.finding: The plan describes hoisting FP-3(c) from plan_review_F1.md to the shared preamble (a migration: remove-then-add), but AC1's proof only verifies the rule is prepended in the preamble — no test or check verifies the rule is REMOVED from plan_review_F1.md. This leaves both sections able to satisfy AC1 while the forward-looking rule exists in duplicate, contradicting the 'live there once' goal stated in the What section.
findings.location: AC1 (proof: `pytest -k resolve_system_preamble`) and What section ('Verified it lives in plan_review_F1.md today (not E1) -- hoist to the shared preamble')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second assertion to the AC1 proof block, e.g.: 'AND `grep -r "Evaluate the spec AS WRITTEN" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_F1.md` exits non-zero (rule absent from original file).' This mirrors the scripted-check pattern already used in AC2 and makes the migration verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify, in AC3, exactly how and where the pre-sweep baseline is captured and stored (e.g., `pytest --eval-spec > baseline.txt` before any prompt edits, committed or saved as a CI artifact) so the post-sweep comparison is deterministic.
findings.finding: AC3 requires 'eval-spec pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline' but the plan never specifies how or where the pre-sweep baseline is recorded before the sweep begins; without a stored baseline value, the acceptance criterion is unmeasurable at verification time.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step: 'Before editing any prompt file, run `python -m rebar eval --spec eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml --out baseline_eval.json` and commit (or artifact) the result. AC3 passes when post-sweep run >= baseline_eval.json pass-rate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether plan-review-coach.eval.yaml should be added (or explicitly scoped out) for AC3 — the eval harness currently has no baseline for PASS_COACH, so the 'eval-spec pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline' proof does not cover the coach prompt's preamble injection.
findings.finding: AC3 asserts the EVAL-SPEC harness proves reworded prompts keep prior recall for all 6 pass prompts mutated via _resolve_system, but plan-review-coach.eval.yaml does not exist in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ — so the eval harness cannot supply a pre-sweep baseline or a regression gate for the coach prompt.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('Proof: eval-spec pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline')
findings.suggested_fix: Either add a minimal plan-review-coach.eval.yaml (even a two-case discrimination spec) to give AC3 full coverage, or explicitly carve out PASS_COACH from AC3's scope with a stated rationale (e.g. 'coach is advisory-only, no recall regression risk').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit pre-sweep baseline capture step to AC3 — specify the file/location where baseline pass-rate is recorded (e.g. a CLI command and output path) before any criterion file is edited.
findings.finding: AC3 requires 'eval-spec pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline (record both)' but the plan never specifies WHERE or HOW the pre-sweep baseline is captured before the affirmative-framing sweep begins. Without an explicit storage location and capture step, the regression gate cannot be satisfied: after the sweep, there is no authoritative pre-sweep number to compare against.
findings.location: AC3: 'Proof: eval-spec pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline (record both). Regress -> revert.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step: 'Before editing any criterion file, run `rebar prompt eval plan-review-finder --record-baseline eval_specs/ws7_baseline.json`; AC3 proof compares post-sweep run against this committed file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either create `eval_specs/plan-review-coach.eval.yaml` as part of this story (or explicitly scope it out and document the gap), and add a protocol decision for what 'pass-rate >= baseline' means when no prior eval spec exists for a modified prompt.
findings.finding: AC3 requires eval regression over `eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml`, but `plan-review-coach.eval.yaml` does not exist in the repo (directory listing of src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ confirms only: plan-review-container, plan-review-finder, plan-review-isf-finder, plan-review-novelty, plan-review-verifier). The PASS_COACH prompt (plan-review-coach) IS routed through _resolve_system and WILL receive the new preamble, but there is no eval spec to validate behavioral regression. The plan specifies no fallback protocol for a prompt that is modified but has no eval baseline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC4, e.g., `grep -q 'review-time checklist' CONTRIBUTING.md && echo PASS`, so the criterion is mechanically verifiable.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Review-time checklist location documented in CONTRIBUTING.md') has no associated proving command or verification step; it is stated as a bare deliverable with no way to confirm it was completed.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: 'Proof: `grep -q "review-time prompt checklist" CONTRIBUTING.md` exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that hoisting FP-3(c) from plan_review_F1.md to the shared preamble applies that rule to all 40 other passes, enumerate which passes are affected, and confirm the eval regression harness includes test cases that would catch a recall drop from FP-3(c) on non-F1 passes.
findings.finding: The plan hoists FP-3(c) (currently residing only in plan_review_F1.md) into the shared preamble, meaning all 40 other reviewer passes will receive a rule they did not previously have. This behavioral change on non-F1 passes is not acknowledged, and the callers (the other 40 pass reviewers) that depend on the old behavior of `_resolve_system` are not enumerated or assessed for compatibility with the new rule.
findings.location: What section — FP-3(c) forward-looking rule / AC: shared preamble injection
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the What section listing which reviewer passes (if any) previously had independent coverage of FP-3(c) or its equivalent, and add at least one eval-spec case per affected pass type that exercises the forward-looking rule so the regression gate is sensitive to it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the eval-spec harness cited in AC3 actually exercises the 41 criterion-level prompt files being modified, or add criterion-targeted eval cases / an alternate proof mechanism that directly validates recall in the reworded criterion prompts.
findings.finding: AC3 asserts the 'EVAL-SPEC harness (eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml)' will validate that 'reworded prompts keep prior recall,' but the existing eval spec files (plan-review-finder, plan-review-verifier, plan-review-container, plan-review-novelty, plan-review-isf-finder) test PASS-LEVEL orchestration prompts — not the 41 individual criterion files (plan_review_T8.md, plan_review_G1G2.md, etc.) that are the actual sweep targets. No eval spec exercises a criterion prompt directly; recall regressions inside the reworded criterion files would be invisible to this harness.
findings.location: AC3: 'Eval/sidecar regression on the EVAL-SPEC harness (eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml): reworded prompts keep prior recall.'
findings.suggested_fix: Either add eval cases to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml that are tagged per-criterion (so T8, G1G2, etc. recall can be measured separately), or create a lightweight criterion-level spot-eval (analogous to fidelity_spot_eval) that probes each modified criterion file against a golden input/output pair before and after the sweep.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify at least the key items the CONTRIBUTING.md review-time checklist must contain (e.g., 'lead affirmative, terse prohibition with adjacent do-this-instead, no bare DO-NOT-only blocks') so the AC is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The CONTRIBUTING.md review-time checklist content is entirely unspecified — the plan names only the file location but not what the checklist should say, leaving the executor to invent it without guidance.
findings.location: What section — R-6 organizing principle; Acceptance Criteria — AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the scripted bare-DO-NOT-only-block check's location (e.g. `tests/unit/test_prompt_hygiene.py`), its detection rule (e.g. regex pattern or AST walk over bullet text), and its CI integration point so the proof is re-runnable as claimed.
findings.finding: AC2 requires 'a scripted check counts bare-DO-NOT-only blocks == 0 across the 41 files' but the plan never specifies the script's location, invocation command, integration point (CI hook vs. standalone), or output format. This is a processing protocol referenced by the AC but not co-located with any schema or concrete specification an implementor can act on.
findings.location: AC2 / What section ('bare DO-NOT-only block' DEFINED)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Implemented as `pytest -k prompt_hygiene_bare_donot` in `tests/unit/test_prompt_hygiene.py`; detection rule: a markdown bullet/paragraph matching `^\s*[-*]\s*(do not|don't|never|avoid)` with no immediately adjacent bullet containing an affirmative alternative.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the architectural decision (shared preamble injected via _resolve_system as the single canonical seam) in CLAUDE.md or a lightweight ADR so future contributors know where cross-cutting prompt directives live and why.
findings.finding: The shared-preamble injection at passes.py:237 introduces a new architectural pattern (a centrally-injected cross-cutting prompt contract), but the plan does not call for capturing this decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update — only CONTRIBUTING.md (for the review-time checklist habit) is mentioned.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #4 / What section (composition point)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short ADR entry or CLAUDE.md section titled 'Shared Prompt Preamble Pattern' that records: (1) the decision — all cross-cutting reviewer directives are injected once in _resolve_system; (2) the rationale — avoids per-file duplication and drift; (3) the consequence — new cross-cutting directives must go here, not in individual prompt files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either scope AC3's regression proof to the 4 existing eval specs (and explicitly acknowledge coach/completion/novelty are not covered) or add a note that those passes require a different validation approach (e.g. spot-eval or human review) before the sweep touches them.
findings.finding: AC3 requires regression via 'the EVAL-SPEC harness (eval_specs/plan-review-*.eval.yaml)', but no eval specs exist for `plan-review-coach`, `plan-review-completion`, or `plan-review-novelty` — three passes that ARE called through `_resolve_system` and will receive the injected preamble. Only 4 eval specs exist: finder, verifier, isf-finder, container. The AC3 regression proof cannot cover the majority of `_resolve_system`-routed passes.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
